mentions.note: Medical devices; essential to healthcare portfolios; solid diversifier.
Preferred medical device play over GE Healthcare; solid compounder in sector.
Medical device leader at new lows; no traction in current market environment obsessed with AI infrastructure.
Long-term winner turned short-term loser, down 23% YTD; can't recommend aggressively until seeing Wednesday quarter as medical device group remains weak.
Down 23% YTD, yields 4%, trades 13x earnings with low single-digit growth. Once-revered company now disfavored in capital allocation shift toward data center.
Medical device sector weakness; entire device cohort struggling and hard to own.
CEO delivered on every promise from last year's conference; AI colonoscopy, Hugo robot, and pulse field ablation are all inflecting as growth drivers.

Post-diabetes spinoff focus on cardiovascular (accelerating), neuroscience, surgical divisions. Cheap valuation (16x FY26, <15x FY27), 3.2% yield, recession-proof profile, mid-to-high single-digit growth. Cramer most bullish in ages.
Pulled back to high 80s at 15x earnings; not war-sensitive; caller Tony averaging down multiple times; I'd buy more at this level.
Eric's portfolio; Cramer would swap for J&J; thinks J&J will have better growth; healthcare exposure but J&J superior.
